user_query:
You are engaged to stand up an Enterprise Risk Management program for our company. Read the following inputs from input/:
- input/org_context.yaml — company profile (industry, size, markets, strategic objectives, current period, recent incidents summary)
- input/risk_appetite.yaml — category-by-category appetite/tolerance thresholds
- input/incidents.jsonl — historic incidents and near misses with categories, dates, and impacts
- input/processes.md — overview of our critical processes and value chain

Deliver the following ERM artifacts under output/ using only text-based files:
1) output/risk_register.yaml — Build a comprehensive risk register with at least 12 distinct risks covering all eight categories (Strategic, Financial, Operational, Compliance, Cyber, Reputational, People, External). For each risk, include all fields in the following structure and rules:
   - id (format: "R-XXX"), title, category, description, cause, consequence, affected_objectives, owner, identified_date (YYYY-MM-DD)
   - inherent_likelihood (1-5), inherent_impact (1-5), inherent_score (must equal likelihood×impact), inherent_rating derived by matrix (Low for 1-5, Medium for 6-11, High for 12-19, Critical for 20-25)
   - controls (at least one; type must be one of Preventive/Detective/Corrective/Directive with effectiveness score)
   - residual_likelihood (1-5), residual_impact (1-5), residual_score (= product), residual_rating (same rating rules)
   - treatment_strategy (Accept/Mitigate/Transfer/Avoid), action_plans (with action, owner, deadline YYYY-MM-DD, status, cost)
   - key_risk_indicators (at least one per risk with green/amber/red thresholds and frequency)
   - review_date (YYYY-MM-DD), trend, velocity
   - outside_appetite: true/false (determine using input/risk_appetite.yaml)
   Additional rules:
   - At least one risk per category
   - At least 3 risks must have residual_rating High or Critical and outside_appetite=true
   - For every High or Critical risk, the controls list must collectively include at least one Preventive, one Detective, and one Corrective control
   - All IDs must be unique and dates must be valid ISO format

2) output/risk_interconnections.yaml — Map interconnections for all risks with residual_rating High or Critical. Use this structure:
   risk_interconnections:
     - primary_risk: "R-XXX"
       connected_risks:
         - risk: "R-YYY"
           relationship: "causes|contributes_to|exacerbates"
           strength: "strong|moderate|weak"
       cascade_scenario: "1-3 sentence domino-effect narrative"
   Include at least two distinct cascade_scenario narratives overall.

3) output/kri_dashboard.yaml — Create a KRI dashboard for the period specified by org_context.yaml (use the exact current period string). Include at least 20 total KRIs across categories with thresholds and a filled summary. Use this structure:
   kri_dashboard:
     period: "YYYY-MM"
     overall_risk_posture: "Green|Amber|Red"
     summary:
       total_kris: N
       green: N
       amber: N
       red: N
       trending_worse: N
       new_breaches: N
     critical_alerts: [list at least 2 if any red]
     category_summary:
       strategic: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }
       financial: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }
       operational: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }
       compliance: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }
       cyber: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }
       people: { green: N, amber: N, red: N }

4) output/board_risk_report.md — A clear, executive-ready narrative report that includes:
   - Top 5 risks with rationale and current residual scores/ratings
   - Appetite breaches and required decisions
   - Mitigation progress and budget/timeline highlights
   - Brief scenario/stress test summary with key gaps and next steps

5) output/scenario_tests.yaml — At least two scenarios using this template fields: name, category, narrative, trigger, timeline, severity, impacts (financial/operational/reputational/regulatory), current_preparedness (existing_controls, gaps_identified, response_plan_status), and recommended_actions (with priority and timeline).

Use a 5×5 likelihood×impact scoring model with ratings derived as: Low (1-5), Medium (6-11), High (12-19), Critical (20-25). Ensure all arithmetic is correct, the outside_appetite flag is set using input/risk_appetite.yaml, and the KRI dashboard period exactly matches org_context.yaml. Where appropriate, leverage the incidents and processes to ground risks, controls, and KRIs in our real context.
